# Baseline Arm — Plain Claude — Profile C2 (Medtech / Health)

**Run date:** 2026-06-28
**Method:** Plain Claude prompt — no SKILL.md, no backbone, no live web search
**Prompt used:**
> "You are a helpful assistant. Which companies in Baden-Württemberg could a student with
> this profile write a Master's thesis at? Profile: interests in medical imaging, biosensors,
> and point-of-care diagnostics; domain is healthcare and medical devices; engineering/empirical
> thesis style (working prototype or validated algorithm as deliverable); skills include Python,
> MATLAB, image processing; no-gos: pure clinical patient-contact without engineering component,
> pharmaceutical synthesis."

---

## Response (plain Claude, no structured skill execution, no backbone reference)

For a student interested in medical imaging, biosensors, and point-of-care diagnostics in Baden-Württemberg, these companies are strong thesis candidates:

**Established medtech corporates:**

1. **Carl Zeiss AG (Oberkochen)** — World leader in optics and medical imaging (OCT, surgical microscopes, ophthalmology systems via Zeiss Meditec). Excellent thesis opportunities in imaging systems engineering. Apply via their career portal.

2. **Karl Storz GmbH (Tuttlingen)** — Global leader in minimally invasive surgery endoscopes. Strong R&D in surgical imaging and, since 2024, expanding into robotics. Check their careers page for thesis listings.

3. **Robert Bosch GmbH (Stuttgart)** — Bosch has a healthcare division and extensive sensor technology research. The Bosch Sensortec subsidiary in Reutlingen focuses on MEMS sensors relevant to health monitoring.

**Diagnostics and life sciences:**

4. **Roche Diagnostics (Mannheim)** — Major diagnostics R&D center in Mannheim. They regularly offer Master's thesis positions in diagnostic device development, biosensors, and point-of-care testing. Note: Mannheim is in BW (not adjacent — it is within BW state boundaries).

**Note:** Baden-Württemberg also has a strong medtech cluster in Tuttlingen beyond Karl Storz — many smaller companies that are not widely known online may also offer thesis opportunities through direct outreach. I'd recommend contacting your university's industry liaison office for regional medtech company contacts.

---

*Note on execution:* Plain Claude responded from parametric memory knowledge. No structured backbone filter was applied. No live web search was run. Companies named are well-known BW medtech actors from training data. Smaller or newer companies (eye2you, Heidelberg Engineering, Medicalvalues, NODE Robotics, Paul Hartmann) were not surfaced.
